children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: There shall be allowed as a deduction all interest paid or accrued within the taxable year on indebtedness.

children.heading: Installment purchases where interest charge is not separately stated
children.label: b Installment purchases where interest charge is not separately stated
A
children.text: which provides that payment of part or all of the purchase price is to be made in installments, and

children.label: B
children.text: in which carrying charges are separately stated but the interest charge cannot be ascertained,

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: In the case of any contract to which paragraph (1) applies, the amount treated as interest for any taxable year shall not exceed the aggregate carrying charges which are properly attributable to such taxable year.

children.heading: Redeemable ground rents
children.label: c Redeemable ground rents
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, any annual or periodic rental under a redeemable ground rent (excluding amounts in redemption thereof) shall be treated as interest on an indebtedness secured by a mortgage.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer other than a corporation, the amount allowed as a deduction under this chapter for investment interest for any taxable year shall not exceed the net investment income of the taxpayer for the taxable year.

children.heading: Carryforward of disallowed interest
children.label: 2 Carryforward of disallowed interest
children.text: The amount not allowed as a deduction for any taxable year by reason of paragraph (1) shall be treated as investment interest paid or accrued by the taxpayer in the succeeding taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “investment interest” means any interest allowable as a deduction under this chapter (determined without regard to paragraph (1)) which is paid or accrued on indebtedness properly allocable to property held for investment.

children.label: i
children.text: any qualified residence interest (as defined in subsection (h)(3)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: any interest which is taken into account under section 469 in computing income or loss from a passive activity of the taxpayer.

children.heading: Personal property used in short sale
children.label: C Personal property used in short sale
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “interest” includes any amount allowable as a deduction in connection with personal property used in a short sale.

children.label: i
children.text: investment income, over

children.label: ii
children.text: investment expenses.

children.label: i
children.text: gross income from property held for investment (other than any gain taken into account under clause (ii)(I)),

children.label: I
children.text: the net gain attributable to the disposition of property held for investment, over

children.label: II
children.text: the net capital gain determined by only taking into account gains and losses from dispositions of property held for investment, plus

children.label: iii
children.text: so much of the net capital gain referred to in clause (ii)(II) (or, if lesser, the net gain referred to in clause (ii)(I)) as the taxpayer elects to take into account under this clause.

children.heading: Investment expenses
children.label: C Investment expenses
children.text: The term “investment expenses” means the deductions allowed under this chapter (other than for interest) which are directly connected with the production of investment income.

children.heading: Income and expenses from passive activities
children.label: D Income and expenses from passive activities
children.text: Investment income and investment expenses shall not include any income or expenses taken into account under section 469 in computing income or loss from a passive activity.

children.label: i
children.text: any property which produces income of a type described in section 469(e)(1), and

children.label: I
children.text: which is not a passive activity, and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to which the taxpayer does not materially participate.

children.heading: Investment expenses
children.label: B Investment expenses
children.text: In the case of property described in subparagraph (A)(i), expenses shall be allocated to such property in the same manner as under section 469.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: C Terms
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the terms “activity”, “passive activity”, and “materially participate” have the meanings given such terms by section 469.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The portion of the original issue discount with respect to any debt instrument which is allowable as a deduction to the issuer for any taxable year shall be equal to the aggregate daily portions of the original issue discount for days during such taxable year.

children.heading: Debt instrument
children.label: A Debt instrument
children.text: The term “debt instrument” has the meaning given such term by section 1275(a)(1).

children.heading: Daily portions
children.label: B Daily portions
children.text: The daily portion of the original issue discount for any day shall be determined under section 1272(a) (without regard to paragraph (7) thereof and without regard to section 1273(a)(3)).

children.heading: Short-term obligations
children.label: C Short-term obligations
children.text: In the case of an obligor of a short-term obligation (as defined in section 1283(a)(1)(A)) who uses the cash receipts and disbursements method of accounting, the original issue discount (and any other interest payable) on such obligation shall be deductible only when paid.

children.heading: Special rule for original issue discount on obligation held by related foreign person
children.label: 3 Special rule for original issue discount on obligation held by related foreign person

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If any debt instrument having original issue discount is held by a related foreign person, any portion of such original issue discount shall not be allowable as a deduction to the issuer until paid. The preceding sentence shall not apply to the extent that the original issue discount is effectively connected with the conduct by such foreign related person of a trade or business within the United States unless such original issue discount is exempt from taxation (or is subject to a reduced rate of tax) pursuant to a treaty obligation of the United States.

children.heading: Special rule for certain foreign entities
children.label: B Special rule for certain foreign entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of any debt instrument having original issue discount which is held by a related foreign person which is a controlled foreign corporation (as defined in section 957) or a passive foreign investment company (as defined in section 1297), a deduction shall be allowable to the issuer with respect to such original issue discount for any taxable year before the taxable year in which paid only to the extent such original issue discount is includible (determined without regard to properly allocable deductions and qualified deficits under section 952(c)(1)(B)) during such prior taxable year in the gross income of a United States person who owns (within the meaning of section 958(a)) stock in such corporation.

children.heading: Secretarial authority
children.label: ii Secretarial authority
children.text: The Secretary may by regulation exempt transactions from the application of clause (i), including any transaction which is entered into by a payor in the ordinary course of a trade or business in which the payor is predominantly engaged.

children.label: i
children.text: who is not a United States person, and

children.label: ii
children.text: who is related (within the meaning of section 267(b)) to the issuer.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 4 Exception
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any debt instrument described in section 1272(a)(2)(D) (relating to loans between natural persons).

children.heading: Special rules for original issue discount on certain high yield obligations
children.label: 5 Special rules for original issue discount on certain high yield obligations
i

children.label: ii
children.text: the remainder of such original issue discount shall not be allowable as a deduction until paid.

children.heading: Disqualified portion treated as stock distribution for purposes of dividend received deduction
children.label: B Disqualified portion treated as stock distribution for purposes of dividend received deduction

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Solely for purposes of sections 243, 245, 246, and 246A, the dividend equivalent portion of any amount includible in gross income of a corporation under section 1272(a) in respect of an applicable high yield discount obligation shall be treated as a dividend received by such corporation from the corporation issuing such obligation.

children.label: I
children.text: which is attributable to the disqualified portion of the original issue discount on such obligation, and

children.label: II
children.text: which would have been treated as a dividend if it had been a distribution made by the issuing corporation with respect to stock in such corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of such original issue discount, or

children.label: II
children.text: the portion of the total return on such obligation which bears the same ratio to such total return as the disqualified yield on such obligation bears to the yield to maturity on such obligation.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: ii Definitions
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “disqualified yield” means the excess of the yield to maturity on the obligation over the sum referred to in subsection (i)(1)(B) plus 1 percentage point, and the term “total return” is the amount which would have been the original issue discount on the obligation if interest described in the parenthetical in section 1273(a)(2) were included in the stated redemption price at maturity.

children.heading: Exception for S corporations
children.label: D Exception for S corporations
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any obligation issued by any corporation for any period for which such corporation is an S corporation.

children.heading: Effect on earnings and profits
children.label: E Effect on earnings and profits
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply for purposes of determining earnings and profits; except that, for purposes of determining the dividend equivalent portion of any amount includible in gross income under section 1272(a) in respect of an applicable high yield discount obligation, no reduction shall be made for any amount attributable to the disqualified portion of any original issue discount on such obligation.

children.heading: Suspension of application of paragraph
children.label: F Suspension of application of paragraph

children.heading: Temporary suspension
children.label: i Temporary suspension
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any applicable high yield discount obligation issued during the period beginning on , and ending on , in exchange (including an exchange resulting from a modification of the debt instrument) for an obligation which is not an applicable high yield discount obligation and the issuer (or obligor) of which is the same as the issuer (or obligor) of such applicable high yield discount obligation. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any obligation the interest on which is interest described in section 871(h)(4) (without regard to subparagraph (D) thereof) or to any obligation issued to a related person (within the meaning of section 108(e)(4)).September 1, 2008December 31, 2009

children.heading: Successive application
children.label: ii Successive application
children.text: Any obligation to which clause (i) applies shall not be treated as an applicable high yield discount obligation for purposes of applying this subparagraph to any other obligation issued in exchange for such obligation.

children.heading: Secretarial authority to suspend application
children.label: iii Secretarial authority to suspend application
children.text: The Secretary may apply this paragraph with respect to debt instruments issued in periods following the period described in clause (i) if the Secretary determines that such application is appropriate in light of distressed conditions in the debt capital markets.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: G Cross reference
children.text: For definition of applicable high yield discount obligation, see subsection (i).

children.heading: Cross references
children.label: 6 Cross references
children.text: For provision relating to deduction of original issue discount on tax-exempt obligation, see section 1288.For special rules in the case of the borrower under certain loans for personal use, see section 1275(b).

children.heading: Denial of deduction for interest on certain obligations not in registered form
children.label: f Denial of deduction for interest on certain obligations not in registered form

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in subsection (a) or in any other provision of law shall be construed to provide a deduction for interest on any registration-required obligation unless such obligation is in registered form.

children.label: i
children.text: is issued by a natural person,

children.label: ii
children.text: is not of a type offered to the public, or

children.label: iii
children.text: has a maturity (at issue) of not more than 1 year.

children.label: B Authority to include other obligations
i
children.text: such obligation is of a type which the Secretary has determined by regulations to be used frequently in avoiding Federal taxes, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such obligation is issued after the date on which the regulations referred to in clause (i) take effect.

children.heading: Book entries permitted, etc.
children.label: 3 Book entries permitted, etc.
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, rules similar to the rules of section 149(a)(3) shall apply, except that a dematerialized book entry system or other book entry system specified by the Secretary shall be treated as a book entry system described in such section.

children.heading: Reduction of deduction where section 25 credit taken
children.label: g Reduction of deduction where section 25 credit taken
children.text: The amount of the deduction under this section for interest paid or accrued during any taxable year on indebtedness with respect to which a mortgage credit certificate has been issued under section 25 shall be reduced by the amount of the credit allowable with respect to such interest under section 25 (determined without regard to section 26).

children.heading: Disallowance of deduction for personal interest
children.label: h Disallowance of deduction for personal interest

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer other than a corporation, no deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for personal interest paid or accrued during the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: interest paid or accrued on indebtedness properly allocable to a trade or business (other than the trade or business of performing services as an employee),

children.label: B
children.text: any investment interest (within the meaning of subsection (d)),

children.label: C
children.text: any interest which is taken into account under section 469 in computing income or loss from a passive activity of the taxpayer,

children.label: D
children.text: any qualified residence interest (within the meaning of paragraph (3)),

children.label: E
children.text: any interest payable under section 6601 on any unpaid portion of the tax imposed by section 2001 for the period during which an extension of time for payment of such tax is in effect under section 6163, and

children.label: ii $1,000,000 limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount treated as acquisition indebtedness for any period shall not exceed $1,000,000 ($500,000 in the case of a married individual filing a separate return).

children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount treated as home equity indebtedness for any period shall not exceed $100,000 ($50,000 in the case of a separate return by a married individual).

children.text: The limitation of subparagraph (B)(ii) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the aggregate amount of outstanding pre-, indebtedness.October 13, 1987

children.label: iv Limitation on period of refinancing
I

children.heading: Mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest
children.label: E Mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Premiums paid or accrued for qualified mortgage insurance by a taxpayer during the taxable year in connection with acquisition indebtedness with respect to a qualified residence of the taxpayer shall be treated for purposes of this section as interest which is qualified residence interest.

children.heading: Phaseout
children.label: ii Phaseout
children.text: The amount otherwise treated as interest under clause (i) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by 10 percent of such amount for each $1,000 ($500 in the case of a married individual filing a separate return) (or fraction thereof) that the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for the taxable year exceeds $100,000 ($50,000 in the case of a married individual filing a separate return).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iii Limitation
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to any mortgage insurance contracts issued before .January 1, 2007

children.label: F Special rules for taxable years beginning after 2017

children.label: I Disallowance of home equity indebtedness interest
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply.

children.label: II Limitation on acquisition indebtedness
children.text: Subparagraph (B)(ii) shall be applied by substituting “$750,000 ($375,000” for “$1,000,000 ($500,000”.

children.label: III Mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest
children.text: Clause (iv) of subparagraph (E) shall not apply.

children.heading: Binding contract exception
children.label: V Binding contract exception
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer who enters into a written binding contract before , to close on the purchase of a principal residence before , and who purchases such residence before , subclause (IV) shall be applied by substituting “” for “”.December 15, 2017January 1, 2018April 1, 2018April 1, 2018December 15, 2017

children.heading: Treatment of refinancings of indebtedness
children.label: ii Treatment of refinancings of indebtedness

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In the case of any indebtedness which is incurred to refinance indebtedness, such refinanced indebtedness shall be treated for purposes of clause (i)(III) as incurred on the date that the original indebtedness was incurred to the extent the amount of the indebtedness resulting from such refinancing does not exceed the amount of the refinanced indebtedness.

children.heading: Limitation on period of refinancing
children.label: II Limitation on period of refinancing
children.text: Subclause (I) shall not apply to any indebtedness after the expiration of the term of the original indebtedness or, if the principal of such original indebtedness is not amortized over its term, the expiration of the term of the 1st refinancing of such indebtedness (or if earlier, the date which is 30 years after the date of such 1st refinancing).

children.heading: Coordination with exclusion of income from discharge of indebtedness
children.label: iii Coordination with exclusion of income from discharge of indebtedness
children.text: Section 108(h)(2) shall be applied without regard to this subparagraph.

children.heading: Special rules for taxable years 2025 through 2028 relating to qualified passenger vehicle loan interest
children.label: 4 Special rules for taxable years 2025 through 2028 relating to qualified passenger vehicle loan interest

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of taxable years beginning after , and before , for purposes of this subsection the term “personal interest” shall not include qualified passenger vehicle loan interest.December 31, 2024January 1, 2029

children.heading: Qualified passenger vehicle loan interest defined
children.label: B Qualified passenger vehicle loan interest defined

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified passenger vehicle loan interest” means any interest which is paid or accrued during the taxable year on indebtedness incurred by the taxpayer after , for the purchase of, and that is secured by a first lien on, an applicable passenger vehicle for personal use.December 31, 2024

children.label: I
children.text: A loan to finance fleet sales.

children.label: II
children.text: A loan incurred for the purchase of a commercial vehicle that is not used for personal purposes.

children.label: III
children.text: Any lease financing.

children.label: IV
children.text: A loan to finance the purchase of a vehicle with a salvage title.

children.label: V
children.text: A loan to finance the purchase of a vehicle intended to be used for scrap or parts.

children.heading: VIN requirement
children.label: iii VIN requirement
children.text: Interest shall not be treated as qualified passenger vehicle loan interest under this paragraph unless the taxpayer includes the vehicle identification number of the applicable passenger vehicle described in clause (i) on the return of tax for the taxable year.

children.heading: Dollar limit
children.label: i Dollar limit
children.text: The amount of interest taken into account by a taxpayer under subparagraph (B) for any taxable year shall not exceed $10,000.

children.heading: Limitation based on modified adjusted gross income
children.label: ii Limitation based on modified adjusted gross income

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The amount which is otherwise allowable as a deduction under subsection (a) as qualified passenger vehicle loan interest (determined without regard to this clause and after the application of clause (i)) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $200 for each $1,000 (or portion thereof) by which the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year exceeds $100,000 ($200,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Modified adjusted gross income
children.label: II Modified adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of this clause, the term “modified adjusted gross income” means the adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.label: i

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: E Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Final assembly
children.label: i Final assembly
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (D), the term “final assembly” means the process by which a manufacturer produces a vehicle at, or through the use of, a plant, factory, or other place from which the vehicle is delivered to a dealer with all component parts necessary for the mechanical operation of the vehicle included with the vehicle, whether or not the component parts are permanently installed in or on the vehicle.

children.heading: Treatment of refinancing
children.label: ii Treatment of refinancing
children.text: Indebtedness described in subparagraph (B) shall include indebtedness that results from refinancing any indebtedness described in such subparagraph, and that is secured by a first lien on the applicable passenger vehicle with respect to which the refinanced indebtedness was incurred, but only to the extent the amount of such resulting indebtedness does not exceed the amount of such refinanced indebtedness.

children.heading: Related parties
children.label: iii Related parties
children.text: Indebtedness described in subparagraph (B) shall not include any indebtedness owed to a person who is related (within the meaning of section 267(b) or 707(b)(1)) to the taxpayer.

children.heading: Residence not rented
children.label: iii Residence not rented
children.text: For purposes of clause (i)(II), notwithstanding section 280A(d)(1), if the taxpayer does not rent a dwelling unit at any time during a taxable year, such unit may be treated as a residence for such taxable year.

children.heading: Special rule for cooperative housing corporations
children.label: B Special rule for cooperative housing corporations
children.text: Any indebtedness secured by stock held by the taxpayer as a tenant-stockholder (as defined in section 216) in a cooperative housing corporation (as so defined) shall be treated as secured by the house or apartment which the taxpayer is entitled to occupy as such a tenant-stockholder. If stock described in the preceding sentence may not be used to secure indebtedness, indebtedness shall be treated as so secured if the taxpayer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that such indebtedness was incurred to acquire such stock.

children.heading: Unenforceable security interests
children.label: C Unenforceable security interests
children.text: Indebtedness shall not fail to be treated as secured by any property solely because, under any applicable State or local homestead or other debtor protection law in effect on , the security interest is ineffective or the enforceability of the security interest is restricted.August 16, 1986

children.heading: Special rules for estates and trusts
children.label: D Special rules for estates and trusts
children.text: For purposes of determining whether any interest paid or accrued by an estate or trust is qualified residence interest, any residence held by such estate or trust shall be treated as a qualified residence of such estate or trust if such estate or trust establishes that such residence is a qualified residence of a beneficiary who has a present interest in such estate or trust or an interest in the residuary of such estate or trust.

children.label: i

children.heading: Special rules for prepaid qualified mortgage insurance
children.label: F Special rules for prepaid qualified mortgage insurance
children.text: Any amount paid by the taxpayer for qualified mortgage insurance that is properly allocable to any mortgage the payment of which extends to periods that are after the close of the taxable year in which such amount is paid shall be chargeable to capital account and shall be treated as paid in such periods to which so allocated. No deduction shall be allowed for the unamortized balance of such account if such mortgage is satisfied before the end of its term. The preceding sentences shall not apply to amounts paid for qualified mortgage insurance provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs or the Rural Housing Service.

children.heading: Carryforward of disallowed business interest
children.label: 2 Carryforward of disallowed business interest
children.text: The amount of any business interest not allowed as a deduction for any taxable year by reason of paragraph (1) shall be treated as business interest paid or accrued in the succeeding taxable year.

children.heading: Exemption for certain small businesses
children.label: 3 Exemption for certain small businesses
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer (other than a tax shelter prohibited from using the cash receipts and disbursements method of accounting under section 448(a)(3)) which meets the gross receipts test of section 448(c) for any taxable year, paragraph (1) shall not apply to such taxpayer for such taxable year. In the case of any taxpayer which is not a corporation or a partnership, the gross receipts test of section 448(c) shall be applied in the same manner as if such taxpayer were a corporation or partnership.

children.text: shall not be treated under paragraph (2) as business interest paid or accrued by the partnership in the succeeding taxable year, and

children.label: II
children.text: shall, subject to clause (ii), be treated as excess business interest which is allocated to each partner in the same manner as the non-separately stated taxable income or loss of the partnership.

children.heading: Treatment of excess business interest allocated to partners
children.label: ii Treatment of excess business interest allocated to partners
I
children.text: such excess business interest shall be treated as business interest paid or accrued by the partner in the next succeeding taxable year in which the partner is allocated excess taxable income from such partnership, but only to the extent of such excess taxable income, and

children.label: II
children.text: any portion of such excess business interest remaining after the application of subclause (I) shall, subject to the limitations of subclause (I), be treated as business interest paid or accrued in succeeding taxable years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The adjusted basis of a partner in a partnership interest shall be reduced (but not below zero) by the amount of excess business interest allocated to the partner under clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Special rule for dispositions
children.label: II Special rule for dispositions
children.text: If a partner disposes of a partnership interest, the adjusted basis of the partner in the partnership interest shall be increased immediately before the disposition by the amount of the excess (if any) of the amount of the basis reduction under subclause (I) over the portion of any excess business interest allocated to the partner under clause (i)(II) which has previously been treated under clause (ii) as business interest paid or accrued by the partner. The preceding sentence shall also apply to transfers of the partnership interest (including by reason of death) in a transaction in which gain is not recognized in whole or in part. No deduction shall be allowed to the transferor or transferee under this chapter for any excess business interest resulting in a basis increase under this subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount determined for the partnership under paragraph (1)(B), over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount (if any) by which the business interest of the partnership, reduced by the floor plan financing interest, exceeds the business interest income of the partnership, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount determined for the partnership under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Application to S corporations
children.label: D Application to S corporations
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (A) and (C) shall apply with respect to any S corporation and its shareholders.

children.heading: Business interest
children.label: 5 Business interest
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “business interest” means any interest paid or accrued on indebtedness properly allocable to a trade or business. Such term shall not include investment interest (within the meaning of subsection (d)). Such term shall not include any interest which is capitalized under section 263(g) or 263A(f).

children.heading: Business interest income
children.label: 6 Business interest income
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “business interest income” means the amount of interest includible in the gross income of the taxpayer for the taxable year which is properly allocable to a trade or business. Such term shall not include investment income (within the meaning of subsection (d)).

children.label: i
children.text: the trade or business of performing services as an employee,

children.label: ii
children.text: any electing real property trade or business,

children.label: iii
children.text: any electing farming business, or

children.label: I
children.text: electrical energy, water, or sewage disposal services,

children.label: II
children.text: gas or steam through a local distribution system, or

children.label: III
children.text: transportation of gas or steam by pipeline,

children.heading: Electing real property trade or business
children.label: B Electing real property trade or business
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “electing real property trade or business” means any trade or business which is described in section 469(c)(7)(C) and which makes an election under this subparagraph. Any such election shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe, and, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.label: i
children.text: a farming business (as defined in section 263A(e)(4)) which makes an election under this subparagraph, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any trade or business of a specified agricultural or horticultural cooperative (as defined in section 199A(g)(2)) with respect to which the cooperative makes an election under this subparagraph.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: any item of income, gain, deduction, or loss which is not properly allocable to a trade or business,

children.label: ii
children.text: any business interest or business interest income,

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of any net operating loss deduction under section 172,

children.label: iv
children.text: the amount of any deduction allowed under section 199A,

children.label: v
children.text: any deduction allowable for depreciation, amortization, or depletion, and

children.label: vi
children.text: the amounts included in gross income under sections 951(a), 951A(a), and 78 (and the portion of the deductions allowed under sections 245A(a) (by reason of section 964(e)(4)) and 250(a)(1)(B) by reason of such inclusions), and

children.label: B
children.text: computed with such other adjustments as provided by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “floor plan financing interest” means interest paid or accrued on floor plan financing indebtedness.

children.label: i
children.text: used to finance the acquisition of motor vehicles held for sale or lease, and

children.label: ii
children.text: secured by the inventory so acquired.

children.label: i
children.text: Any self-propelled vehicle designed for transporting persons or property on a public street, highway, or road.

children.label: ii
children.text: A boat.

children.label: iii
children.text: Farm machinery or equipment.

children.text: the limitation under paragraph (1) shall apply to business interest without regard to whether the taxpayer would otherwise deduct such business interest or capitalize such business interest under an interest capitalization provision, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any reference in this subsection to a deduction for business interest shall be treated as including a reference to the capitalization of business interest.

children.heading: Amount allowed applied first to capitalized interest
children.label: B Amount allowed applied first to capitalized interest
i
children.text: shall be applied first to the aggregate amount of business interest which would otherwise be capitalized, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the remainder (if any) shall be applied to the aggregate amount of business interest which would be deducted.

children.heading: Treatment of disallowed interest carried forward
children.label: C Treatment of disallowed interest carried forward
children.text: No portion of any business interest carried forward under paragraph (2) from any taxable year to any succeeding taxable year shall, for purposes of this title (including any interest capitalization provision which previously applied to such portion) be treated as interest to which an interest capitalization provision applies.

children.label: i
children.text: is required to be charged to capital account, or

children.label: ii
children.text: may be deducted or charged to capital account.

children.heading: Regulatory authority
children.label: 11 Regulatory authority
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or guidance as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations or guidance to determine which business interest is taken into account under this subsection and section 59A(c)(3).

children.heading: Special rule for taxable years beginning in 2019 and 2020
children.label: 12 Special rule for taxable years beginning in 2019 and 2020

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii) or (iii), in the case of any taxable year beginning in 2019 or 2020, paragraph (1)(B) shall be applied by substituting “50 percent” for “30 percent”.

children.label: I
children.text: clause (i) shall not apply to any taxable year beginning in 2019, but

children.label: aa
children.text: 50 percent of such excess business interest shall be treated as business interest which, notwithstanding paragraph (4)(B)(ii), is paid or accrued by the partner in the partner’s first taxable year beginning in 2020 and which is not subject to the limits of paragraph (1), and

children.label: bb
children.text: 50 percent of such excess business interest shall be subject to the limitations of paragraph (4)(B)(ii) in the same manner as any other excess business interest so allocated.

children.heading: Election out
children.label: iii Election out
children.text: A taxpayer may elect, at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe, not to have clause (i) apply to any taxable year. Such an election, once made, may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary. In the case of a partnership, any such election shall be made by the partnership and may be made only for taxable years beginning in 2020.

children.heading: Election to use 2019 adjusted taxable income for taxable years beginning in 2020
children.label: B Election to use 2019 adjusted taxable income for taxable years beginning in 2020

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), in the case of any taxable year beginning in 2020, the taxpayer may elect to apply this subsection by substituting the adjusted taxable income of the taxpayer for the last taxable year beginning in 2019 for the adjusted taxable income for such taxable year. In the case of a partnership, any such election shall be made by the partnership.

children.heading: Special rule for short taxable years
children.label: ii Special rule for short taxable years
children.text: If an election is made under clause (i) for a taxable year which is a short taxable year, the adjusted taxable income for the taxpayer’s last taxable year beginning in 2019 which is substituted under clause (i) shall be equal to the amount which bears the same ratio to such adjusted taxable income determined without regard to this clause as the number of months in the short taxable year bears to 122So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.2

children.label: A
children.text: For requirement that an electing real property trade or business use the alternative depreciation system, see section 168(g)(1)(F).

children.label: B
children.text: For requirement that an electing farming business use the alternative depreciation system, see section 168(g)(1)(G).

children.heading: Section 6166 interest
children.label: k Section 6166 interest
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this section for any interest payable under section 6601 on any unpaid portion of the tax imposed by section 2001 for the period during which an extension of time for payment of such tax is in effect under section 6166.

children.heading: Disallowance of deduction on certain debt instruments of corporations
children.label: l Disallowance of deduction on certain debt instruments of corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for any interest paid or accrued on a disqualified debt instrument.

children.heading: Disqualified debt instrument
children.label: 2 Disqualified debt instrument
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “disqualified debt instrument” means any indebtedness of a corporation which is payable in equity of the issuer or a related party or equity held by the issuer (or any related party) in any other person.

children.heading: Special rules for amounts payable in equity
children.label: 3 Special rules for amounts payable in equity
A
children.text: a substantial amount of the principal or interest is required to be paid or converted, or at the option of the issuer or a related party is payable in, or convertible into, such equity,

children.label: B
children.text: a substantial amount of the principal or interest is required to be determined, or at the option of the issuer or a related party is determined, by reference to the value of such equity, or

children.label: C
children.text: the indebtedness is part of an arrangement which is reasonably expected to result in a transaction described in subparagraph (A) or (B).

children.heading: Capitalization allowed with respect to equity of persons other than issuer and related parties
children.label: 4 Capitalization allowed with respect to equity of persons other than issuer and related parties
children.text: If the disqualified debt instrument of a corporation is payable in equity held by the issuer (or any related party) in any other person (other than a related party), the basis of such equity shall be increased by the amount not allowed as a deduction by reason of paragraph (1) with respect to the instrument.

children.heading: Exception for certain instruments issued by dealers in securities
children.label: 5 Exception for certain instruments issued by dealers in securities
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “disqualified debt instrument” does not include indebtedness issued by a dealer in securities (or a related party) which is payable in, or by reference to, equity (other than equity of the issuer or a related party) held by such dealer in its capacity as a dealer in securities. For purposes of this paragraph, the term “dealer in securities” has the meaning given such term by section 475.

children.heading: Related party
children.label: 6 Related party
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a person is a related party with respect to another person if such person bears a relationship to such other person described in section 267(b) or 707(b).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 7 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations preventing avoidance of this subsection through the use of an issuer other than a corporation.

children.heading: Interest on unpaid taxes attributable to nondisclosed reportable transactions
children.label: m Interest on unpaid taxes attributable to nondisclosed reportable transactions
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for any interest paid or accrued under section 6601 on any underpayment of tax which is attributable to the portion of any reportable transaction understatement (as defined in section 6662A(b)) with respect to which the requirement of section 6664(d)(2)(A) is not met.1
For disallowance of certain amounts paid in connection with insurance, endowment, or annuity contracts, see section 264.
For disallowance of deduction for interest relating to tax-exempt income, see section 265(a)(2).
For disallowance of deduction for carrying charges chargeable to capital account, see section 266.
For disallowance of interest with respect to transactions between related taxpayers, see section 267.
For treatment of redeemable ground rents and real property held subject to liabilities under redeemable ground rents, see section 1055.
